meta.title: Convert M4A to MP3 — Free, In Your Browser, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Convert .m4a audio to .mp3 right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded — your file never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark, no quality cap.
og.title: Convert M4A to MP3 — Free, No Upload
og.description: Turn .m4a (Apple AAC) audio into a universal .mp3, locally in your browser.
hero.title: Convert M4A to MP3 — right in your browser
hero.lead: Drop an .m4a file — a voice memo, a song, a recording from your phone — and get a plain .mp3 that plays everywhere. It's converted on your own device, so the audio is never uploaded.
prose.p1: M4A is Apple's audio container — it's what iPhone Voice Memos, Apple Music downloads and GarageBand exports use. It's a fine format, but plenty of older car stereos, media players, DJ software and Bluetooth speakers still expect MP3. Converting to MP3 trades a sliver of efficiency for the format that genuinely plays on everything.
prose.p2: Most converters upload your audio to a server first. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't — the conversion runs locally in your browser, so nothing is stored or logged, there's no file-size limit and no queue, and a private recording stays private.
faq.a1: No. The conversion happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — open your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.q2: What MP3 quality do I get?
faq.a2: You can pick the bitrate before converting — from 128 kbps (small) up to 320 kbps (best quality). Since M4A is already compressed, 192 kbps or higher keeps the result indistinguishable for most ears.
faq.q3: Does it keep the title and artist tags?
faq.a3: The audio itself is converted faithfully. Some metadata may not carry across every container, so if exact ID3 tags matter, double-check them in your music app afterwards.
